{{日本の氏族
|家名= Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko
|家紋= <!-- 家紋の画像 -->
|家紋名称= {{ruby|九枚笹|くまいささ}}<br/>{{ruby|熨斗|のし}}<br/>{{ruby|菊|きく}}
|本姓= Ki clan
|家祖= {{ill|Amichine-no-Mikoto|ja|天道根命}}{{Sfn|太田|1934|p=1823}}
|種別= {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}}<br/>　{{ill|Shake|ja|社家}}<br/>　{{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} ({{ill|baron|ja|男爵}})
|出身地= {{ill|Kii Province|ja|紀伊国}}
|根拠地= {{ill|Hinokuma-Kunikakasu Jingū|ja|日前神宮・國懸神宮}} in Kii Province<br/>{{ill|Wakayama, Wakayama|ja|和歌山市|lt=Wakayama|lt_ja=和歌山市}} in {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}
|人物= See [[#People|People]]
|支流= Ōta-no-Hōri, Yama clan, Tanba Tatenui clan, Tsumatakumi clan, Takano clan, {{ill|Ōmura clan (shinbetsu)|ja|大村氏 (神別)|lt=Ōmura clan|lt_ja=大村氏}}, {{ill|Fujitsu-Tatsu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|藤津立国造}}, {{ill|Iwami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石見国造}}, Nyū-no-Hōri, Narahara clan, Osaka clan, {{ill|Inbe clan|ja|忌部氏}}, Jinnu clan, Nyū-Aimi clan
}}

'''Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (''ki no kuni no miyatsuko'', ''kii kokusō'') was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} that ruled {{ill|Kii Province|ja|紀伊国}} (present-day {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}). The kuni no miyatsuko clan was a family descended from the head of the {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} '''Ki clan''', and in ancient times for generations they inherited the {{ill|rituals|ja|祭祀}} of {{ill|Hinokuma-Kunikakasu Jingū|ja|日前神宮・國懸神宮}} together with the {{ill|position of kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造|lt=position of kuni no miyatsuko|lt_ja=国造職}} of Kii Province; and even after the kuni no miyatsuko system was abolished upon the implementation of the {{ill|ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, they continued to call themselves "kuni no miyatsuko" as {{ill|Gūji|ja|宮司}} of these same shrines.

== Overview ==
=== Name ===
In the "Kokuzō Hongi" of the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' it is written as Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko, but anciently it was also written as '''Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (''ki no kuni no miyatsuko'') in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and as '''Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}''.

=== Ancestry ===
*According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" of the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'', '''{{ill|Amichine-no-Mikoto|ja|天道根命}}''', 5th-generation descendant of {{ill|Kamimusubi-no-Mikoto|ja|神皇産霊命}}, was appointed kuni no miyatsuko during the {{ill|Emperor Jinmu|ja|神武天皇|lt=Emperor Jinmu|lt_ja=神武朝}} court. In the ''{{ill|Kii Zoku-Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記}}'' he is also said to be the {{ill|direct lineage|ja|嫡流}} of Amichine-no-Mikoto, who was invested as {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} of Kii after Emperor Jinmu pacified the {{ill|Kinai|ja|畿内}}.
*The first actually-appointed Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko is considered to have been Ki-no-Oshikatsu of the 6th century (''Nihon Shoki'' Emperor Bidatsu Year 12 Month 7 Hinoto-tori entry, Month 10 entry)<ref>{{Cite book|和書|title=古代氏族の系譜を読み解く|date=2022年2月1日|publisher=吉川弘文館|page=134|author=鈴木正信}}</ref>.

=== Clan ===
'''Ki clan''' (''ki-uji''; {{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ|lt=kabane|lt_ja=姓}} of {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君 (カバネ)|lt=Kimi|lt_ja=君}}); later, in the {{ill|Kōgo-Nenjaku|ja|庚午年籍}}, it appears they changed kabane to '''Ki-{{ill|Atai (kabane)|ja|直 (姓)|lt=Atai|lt_ja=直}}'''<ref>{{ill|Hōga Toshio|ja|宝賀寿男}}, "Chapter 1: Tenjin-lineage Clans, Section 6: Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko Clans, 1 Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko Ki-no-Atai, Ki-no-Sukune (1)," ''Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei'' Vol. 2, Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, pp. 879-881.</ref>, and in {{ill|Jōwa (Japan)|ja|承和 (日本)|lt=Jōwa|lt_ja=承和}} 2 Month 3 they were granted the kabane '''Ki-{{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰}}'''.

Even today — including the age of {{ill|mythology|ja|神話}}, over 2,000 years — they still serve the deity of Hinokuma-Kunikakasu. Conveying such an ancient family lineage to the present, {{要出典範囲|date=2017年4月|excluding the {{ill|imperial family|ja|皇室|lt=imperial family|lt_ja=天皇家}}, is said to be limited to only: the Senge and Kitajima families of the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|出雲国造}} house; the {{ill|Aso family|ja|阿蘇氏|lt=Aso family|lt_ja=阿蘇家}} ({{ill|Aso no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿蘇国造}}), {{ill|Ōgūji|ja|大宮司}} of {{ill|Aso Shrine|ja|阿蘇神社}}; the Miyanari and Itōzu families ({{ill|Usa no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造}}), the two Ōgūji families of {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} from the {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏|lt=Usa clan|lt_ja=宇佐氏}}; the {{ill|Oki family|ja|億岐氏|lt=Oki family|lt_ja=億岐家}}, former Oki no Kuni no Miyatsuko house; the {{ill|Amabe family|ja|海部氏|lt=Amabe family|lt_ja=海部家}}, Gūji of {{ill|Kono Shrine|ja|籠神社}}; the Senshū family ({{ill|Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造}}) from the {{ill|Owari clan|ja|尾張氏|lt=Owari clan|lt_ja=尾張氏}}, Ōgūji of {{ill|Atsuta Jingū|ja|熱田神宮}}; the {{ill|Tsumori family|ja|津守氏|lt=Tsumori family|lt_ja=津守家}}, Gūji of {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}}; and the {{ill|Suwa family|ja|諏訪氏|lt=Suwa family|lt_ja=諏訪家}} (Miwa clan), Ōhōri of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}}}. Especially together with the Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko, their appointment required authorization from the {{ill|imperial court|ja|朝廷 (日本)|lt=imperial court|lt_ja=朝廷}}<ref>''{{ill|Jōgan Gishiki|ja|貞観儀式}}'' Vol. 10, "Daijōkan Sōshi-chō Ninki no Kuni no Miyatsuko Gi" entry.</ref>. Because of an extinction of the successor line, the succession was switched to a female line twice; the first to inherit the kuni no miyatsuko office was Ki-no-Fumiteru (son of {{ill|Ki-no-Yoshimitsu|ja|紀淑光}}), from the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏}} linked through the female line to the Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko house.

From the time of the 64th {{ill|Ki-no-Toshitsura|ja|紀俊連|lt=Toshitsura|lt_ja=俊連}}, they began to build castles in various places of the shrine domain (such as {{ill|Ōta Castle (Kii Province)|ja|太田城 (紀伊国)|lt=Ōta Castle|lt_ja=太田城}} and {{ill|Akizuki Castle (Kii Province)|ja|秋月城 (紀伊国)|lt=Akizuki Castle|lt_ja=秋月城}}) in preparation for external enemies. Although the Ki clan was {{ill|Shinto priests|ja|神職|lt=Shinto priests|lt_ja=神官}}, they owned territory on the scale of regional {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名}}, and the surrounding {{ill|local strongmen|ja|土豪}} had their eyes on their land. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)|lt=Sengoku period|lt_ja=戦国時代}}, around the time of the 67th {{ill|Ki-no-Tadao|ja|紀忠雄|lt=Tadao|lt_ja=忠雄}}, the {{ill|Saika-shū|ja|雑賀衆}}, whose territory was at times adjacent to the Ki clan's, would occasionally attack those castles. Through {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}'s {{ill|Siege of Kii|ja|紀州征伐}}, the shrine buildings of both Hinokuma and Kunikakasu Jingū were completely destroyed, and the Ki clan's shrine lands were confiscated. Tadao returned to the area after a temporary palace was built by the hand of {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言}} {{ill|Toyotomi Hidenaga|ja|豊臣秀長|lt=Hidenaga|lt_ja=秀長}}. Subsequently, by the order of {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩|lt=Kishū domain lord|lt_ja=紀州藩主}} {{ill|Tokugawa Yorinobu|ja|徳川頼宣}}, the shrine buildings were restored to their original site. In {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} 4 ({{ill|1627|ja|1627年}}), Yorinobu granted the 68th {{ill|Ki-no-Tadamitsu|ja|紀忠光|lt=Tadamitsu|lt_ja=忠光}} 40 ''koku'' of shrine lands.

During the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} and the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} they faced crises of extinction, but in both cases they succeeded through the female line. The 38th {{ill|Ki-no-Tomoyo|ja|紀奉世|lt=Tomoyo|lt_ja=奉世}}'s {{ill|adopted son-in-law|ja|婿養子}} was {{ill|Ki-no-Yukiyoshi|ja|紀行義|lt=Yukiyoshi|lt_ja=行義}} from the {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別}} {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏}}, descendants of {{ill|Takenouchi-no-Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}}; the 74th {{ill|Ki-no-Yoshitoshi|ja|紀慶俊|lt=Yoshitoshi|lt_ja=慶俊}}'s adopted son-in-law was {{ill|Ki-no-Mitsufuyu|ja|紀三冬|lt=Mitsufuyu|lt_ja=三冬}} from the {{ill|Asukai family|ja|飛鳥井家}} of the {{ill|kuge|ja|公家}}. Yukiyoshi was of the {{ill|Ki-no-Haseo|ja|紀長谷雄|lt=Haseo line|lt_ja=長谷雄流}} of the Ki clan, son of '''Fumiteru''' (''fumiteru'' or ''fumiaki''), grandson of {{ill|Yoshimitsu|ja|紀淑光|lt=Yoshimitsu|lt_ja=淑光}}, great-grandson of Haseo. It is said he accompanied his uncle {{ill|Ki-no-Fumitoshi|ja|紀文利|lt=Fumitoshi|lt_ja=文利}} (Fumiteru's younger brother) when Fumitoshi was appointed {{ill|kokushi|ja|国司}} to Kii during the {{ill|Kōhō|ja|康保|lt=Kōhō|lt_ja=康保年間}} era (mid-10th century), and at that time married the daughter of Tomoyo; through this connection, during the {{ill|Tengen (Japan)|ja|天元 (日本)|lt=Tengen|lt_ja=天元年中}} era (late 10th century), he was granted the kuni no miyatsuko office. The Asukai family from which Mitsufuyu came is from the {{ill|Moroie line|ja|師実流}} (Kazan'in line) of the {{ill|Hokke|ja|藤原北家}}; Mitsufuyu was the fourth son of {{ill|Asukai Masashige|ja|飛鳥井雅重|lt=Masashige|lt_ja=雅重}}, and the adoption took place during the {{ill|Bunka (era)|ja|文化 (元号)|lt=Bunka|lt_ja=文化年中}} era (beginning of the 19th century){{Efn2|The mother of Takenouchi-no-Sukune, the founder of the kōbetsu Ki clan, was the younger sister of the 6th kuni no miyatsuko Ujihiko-no-Mikoto; and Mitsufuyu's father-in-law Yoshitoshi's 7-generations-back grandmother (wife of 64th kuni no miyatsuko Toshitsura) was from the Asukai family, so both families already had relations with the kuni no miyatsuko house.}}.

In the generation of {{ill|Ki-no-Toshinao|ja|紀俊尚|lt=Toshinao|lt_ja=俊尚}}, he was enrolled in the {{ill|kazoku|ja|華族}} in Meiji 5{{sfn|華族大鑑刊行会|1990|p=449}}; when the kazoku became a five-rank system with the promulgation of the {{ill|Kazoku Edict|ja|華族令}} on Meiji 17 Month 7 Day 7, on the following day the 8th he was enrolled as a baron{{sfn|小田部雄次|2006|p=339}}. His son {{ill|Ki-no-Toshihide|ja|紀俊秀|lt=Toshihide|lt_ja=俊秀}} was elected five times as baronial member of the House of Peers and served there, belonging to the {{ill|Kōseikai|ja|公正会}}{{sfn|華族大鑑刊行会|1990|p=449}}.

== Generations of Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko ==
{{columns-list|colwidth=25em|
# {{ill|Amichine-no-Mikoto|ja|天道根命}} (''amichine-no-mikoto'')
# {{ill|Hikoma-no-Mikoto|ja|比古麻命}}
# {{ill|Kitone-no-Mikoto|ja|鬼刀禰命}}
# {{ill|Kushitama-no-Mikoto|ja|久志多麻命}}
# {{ill|Ōnakusahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大名草比古命}}
# {{ill|Ujihiko|ja|莵道彦|lt=Ujihiko-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=宇遅比古命}}
# {{ill|Funaki-no-Mikoto|ja|舟木命}}
# {{ill|Yatsukashihiko|ja|夜都加志彦}}
# {{ill|Toyomimi-no-Mikoto|ja|等与美々命}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Toyofuru|ja|紀豊布流}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Shiogoe|ja|紀塩籠}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Nekashiho|ja|紀禰賀志富}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Shinobu|ja|紀忍}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Kunimi|ja|紀国見}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Masate|ja|紀麻佐手}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Kunikatsu|ja|紀国勝}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Oshikatsu|ja|紀忍勝}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Ōumi|ja|紀大海}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Oshiho|ja|紀忍穂}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Muro|ja|紀牟婁}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Iwamu|ja|紀石牟}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Maso|ja|紀摩祖}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Komaro (Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko)|ja|紀古麿 (紀伊国造)|lt=Ki-no-Komaro|lt_ja=紀古麿}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Hayashi-no-Atai|ja|紀林直}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Chishima|ja|紀千嶋}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Tarikuni|ja|紀足国}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Toyoshima|ja|紀豊島}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Yoshitsugu (Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko)|ja|紀吉継 (紀伊国造)|lt=Ki-no-Yoshitsugu|lt_ja=紀吉継}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Yutaka|ja|紀豊}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Iotomo|ja|紀五百友}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Kunisu|ja|紀国栖}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Toyonari|ja|紀豊成}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Takatsugu|ja|紀高継}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Hirofuchi|ja|紀弘淵}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Tsukio|ja|紀槻雄}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Hiroyo|ja|紀広世}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Arimori|ja|紀有守}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Tomoyo|ja|紀奉世}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Yukiyoshi|ja|紀行義}} — grandson of {{ill|Ki-no-Yoshimitsu|ja|紀淑光}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Takatsune|ja|紀孝経}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Yoshitaka|ja|紀義孝}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Takahiro|ja|紀孝弘}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Takanaga|ja|紀孝長}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Takasue|ja|紀孝季}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Tsunesuke|ja|紀経佐}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Yoshimori|ja|紀良守}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Yoshisuke|ja|紀良佐}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Yoshitada|ja|紀良忠}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Yoshihira|ja|紀良平}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Yoshinobu|ja|紀良宣}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Nobutoshi|ja|紀宣俊}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Nobumune|ja|紀宣宗}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Nobuyasu|ja|紀宣保}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Nobuchika|ja|紀宣親}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Yoshifumi|ja|紀淑文}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Yoshiuji|ja|紀淑氏}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Toshifumi|ja|紀俊文}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Chikafumi|ja|紀親文}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Toshinaga|ja|紀俊長}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Yukifumi|ja|紀行文}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Yukinaga|ja|紀行長}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Yukitaka|ja|紀行孝}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Chikahiro|ja|紀親弘}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Toshitsura|ja|紀俊連}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Toshishige|ja|紀俊調}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Mitsuo|ja|紀光雄}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Tadao|ja|紀忠雄}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Tadamitsu|ja|紀忠光}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Masanaga|ja|紀昌長}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Toshihiro|ja|紀俊弘}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Toshinori|ja|紀俊範}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Toyofumi|ja|紀豊文}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Toshiyoshi|ja|紀俊敬}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Yoshitoshi|ja|紀慶俊}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Mitsufuyu|ja|紀三冬}} (Toshiyasu) — fourth son of {{ill|Asukai Masashige|ja|飛鳥井雅重}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Hisanaga|ja|紀尚長}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Toshinao|ja|紀俊尚}} — Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko abolished in the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, enrolled as {{ill|baron|ja|男爵}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Toshihide|ja|紀俊秀}} — {{ill|Mayor of Wakayama|ja|和歌山市|lt=Mayor of Wakayama|lt_ja=和歌山市長}}, {{ill|member of the House of Peers|ja|貴族院 (日本)|lt=member of the House of Peers|lt_ja=貴族院議員}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Toshitada|ja|紀俊忠}} — second son of {{ill|Imazono Kuniteru|ja|今園国映}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Toshiyuki|ja|紀俊行}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Toshiaki|ja|紀俊明}} — current {{ill|head|ja|家長|lt=head|lt_ja=当主}}
# {{ill|Ki-no-Toshihisa|ja|紀俊寿}}
}}

== Footnotes ==
{{脚注ヘルプ}}
=== Notes ===
{{Notelist2}}
=== Citations ===
{{Reflist}}

== References ==
{{脚注の不足|date=2017年4月|section=1}}
* {{オープンアクセス}}{{Citation|和書|last=太田|first=亮|author-link=太田亮|others=[[上田萬年]]・[[三上参次]]監修|chapter=紀　キ|pages=1823-1836|volume=2|date=1934|title=姓氏家系大辞典|publisher=姓氏家系大辞典刊行会|id={{全国書誌番号|47004572}}|url={{NDLDC|1130938/4}}|ncid=BN05000207|oclc=673726070|ref=harv}}
* ''Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko Keizu'', contained in {{ill|Hanawa Hokiichi|ja|塙保己一}} (ed.), ''{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従}}'' Vol. 7 (Genealogy Section) Part 2.
* {{ill|Kōda Toshio|ja|甲田利雄}}, "Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko Keizu Kaidai," in ''Gunsho Kaidai'' Vol. 3 (Genealogy Section) Part 2, Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kanseikai, 1962.
* {{Cite book|和書 |date=1990 |title=華族大鑑 |series=日本人物誌叢書7 |author=華族大鑑刊行会 |publisher=[[日本図書センター]] |isbn=978-4820540342 |ref=harv}}
* {{Cite book|和書 |date=2006 |title=華族 近代日本貴族の虚像と実像 |author=小田部雄次 |authorlink=小田部雄次 |publisher=[[中央公論新社]] |series=[[中公新書]]1836 |isbn=978-4121018366 |ref=harv}}
== See also ==
* {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏}}

== External links ==
* [http://hinokuma-jingu.com/ Hinokuma Jingū / Kunikakasu Jingū Official Site]

{{Shinto-stub}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{リダイレクトの所属カテゴリ
|redirect1=紀家
|1-1= 日本の男爵家
}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:きのくにのみやつこ}}
[[Category:Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|!きのくにのみやつこ]]
[[Category:社家の氏族]]
[[Category:和歌山市の歴史]]
[[Category:紀伊国]]
[[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]]
[[ja:紀伊国造]]
